        stop
      • stop(server: HttpServer)

        writeDisposition
      • writeDisposition(buf: ptr UncheckedArray[byte]; name: string; p: var int)

A non-blocking HTTP/1.1 server built on top of powpow's TCP primitives.

Combines the TCP transport layer with the incremental HTTP parser. Higher-level frameworks implement routing via the OnRequestCallback.

Usage:

let server = newHttpServer()
server.start do (req: HttpRequest, res: HttpResponse):
  if req.getPath() == "/":
    res.status(Http200).send("Hello, World!")
  else:
    res.sendError(Http404, "Not Found")
, Port(9000)

Imports

../net/tcp, ../net/common, ../loop, ../types, http

Types

ConnHttp = ref object
HttpResponse = ref object
Build and send an HTTP response. 
HttpServer = ref object
  handler*: OnRequestCallback
  maxBodySize*: int64
  maxConnections*: int
  maxPipelineDepth*: int
  readTimeoutMs*: int
OnRequestCallback = proc (req: HttpRequest; res: HttpResponse) {....gcsafe.}
User-provided callback invoked for every parsed HTTP request. Higher-level frameworks implement routing on top of this.

Consts

DefaultChunkSize = 1048576
DefaultKeepAliveMs = 5000

Procs

proc addConnection(server: HttpServer; fd: SocketHandle) {.
    ...raises: [Exception, KeyError, OSError], tags: [RootEffect], forbids: [].}
Inject a pre-accepted client fd into this HTTP server's event loop. Used by multi-threaded acceptors that distribute connections to workers. 
proc close(res: HttpResponse): HttpResponse {.discardable, ...raises: [], tags: [],
    forbids: [].}
Mark this response to send "Connection: close" and shut down the TCP connection after the response is sent. 
proc close(server: HttpServer) {....raises: [KeyError], tags: [], forbids: [].}
proc ensureTcpServer(server: HttpServer) {....raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
proc getConn(res: HttpResponse): Connection {.inline, ...raises: [], tags: [],
    forbids: [].}
Get the underlying TCP connection. Used by protocol upgrade handlers (e.g. WebSocket) that need direct access to the socket. 
proc getFileExt(path: string): string {....raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
Extract the lowercase file extension from a path. 
proc getLoop(server: HttpServer): Loop {.inline, ...raises: [], tags: [],
    forbids: [].}
Get the event loop associated with this server. 
proc header(res: HttpResponse; key, value: string): HttpResponse {.discardable,
    ...raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
Add a response header. 
proc listen(server: HttpServer; address: string; port: int) {.
    ...raises: [NetError, KeyError, OSError], tags: [], forbids: [].}
proc listenUnix(server: HttpServer; path: string; mode: int = 0o000000000660) {.
    ...raises: [NetError, KeyError, OSError], tags: [], forbids: [].}
proc markSent(res: HttpResponse) {.inline, ...raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
Mark this response as sent without writing any bytes. Used by upgrade handlers that send the response manually. 
proc newHttpServer(loop: Loop; populate: bool = false): HttpServer {.
    ...raises: [Exception], tags: [RootEffect], forbids: [].}
proc newHttpServer(populate: bool = false): HttpServer {.
    ...raises: [OSError, Exception], tags: [TimeEffect, RootEffect], forbids: [].}
proc parseRange(rangeHeader: string; fileSize: int64): tuple[ok: bool,
    start, length: int64] {....raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
Parse an HTTP Range header (single range, bytes=start-end). Supports explicit ranges (0-1023), open-ended ranges (1024-), and suffix ranges (-500). Clamps rangeEnd to fileSize - 1. Returns (true, startByte, length) on success, (false, 0, 0) on failure. 
proc populatePools(server: HttpServer; poolSize = 256) {....raises: [], tags: [],
    forbids: [].}
proc removeSession(server: HttpServer; conn: Connection) {....raises: [OSError],
    tags: [WriteDirEffect], forbids: [].}
proc send(res: HttpResponse; body: seq[byte]) {....raises: [KeyError, OSError],
    tags: [], forbids: [].}
proc send(res: HttpResponse; body: string = "") {....raises: [KeyError, OSError],
    tags: [], forbids: [].}
proc sendError(res: HttpResponse; code: HttpCode; msg: string = "") {.
    ...raises: [KeyError, OSError], tags: [], forbids: [].}
Send an error response and close the connection. 
proc sendFile(res: HttpResponse; path: string;
              req: HttpRequest = default(HttpRequest); closeConn = true;
              contentDisposition = true; skipRange = false) {.
    ...raises: [KeyError, OSError], tags: [], forbids: [].}
Send a file for download using zero-copy when possible. Adds Content-Disposition: attachment; filename="..." when contentDisposition is true. Supports HTTP Range requests when req is provided. closeConn controls connection lifetime: true (default) closes after the transfer; false keeps the connection alive. skipRange bypasses Range header parsing (used when upper layer already decided Range should not be honored via If-Range). 
proc serveFile(res: HttpResponse; req: HttpRequest; path: string;
               fsRoot: string = ""; contentType: string = "";
               attach: bool = false; etag: string = "";
               lastModified: string = ""; chunkSize: int64 = 0): bool {.
    ...raises: [KeyError, OSError], tags: [TimeEffect], forbids: [].}

High-level static file serving with resume download support. Handles Range, If-Range, If-None-Match, If-Modified-Since, and ETag. Zero-copy: uses sendFile internally (sendfile syscall). Returns true if the file was served, false if file not found.

When etag is empty, a strong ETag is auto-computed from file size and mtime. When fsRoot is set, path traversal attempts are rejected with 403. chunkSize > 0 enables byte-limited streaming for media seeking.

proc serveStatic(res: HttpResponse; req: HttpRequest; urlPrefix: string;
                 fsRoot: string;
                 indexFiles: seq[string] = @["index.html", "index.htm"]): bool {.
    ...raises: [KeyError, OSError], tags: [ReadDirEffect], forbids: [].}
Serve static files from fsRoot for requests whose path starts with urlPrefix. Path traversal protection: rejects paths containing ".." or "~". Returns true if the file was served, false if not found (caller should send 404). Zero-copy: uses sendFile internally, no body buffering. 
proc setKeepAliveTimeout(server: HttpServer; ms: int) {....raises: [], tags: [],
    forbids: [].}
Set the keep-alive idle timeout in milliseconds. 0 disables it. 
proc start(server: HttpServer; handler: OnRequestCallback; port: Port) {.
    ...raises: [Exception], tags: [RootEffect, TimeEffect], forbids: [].}
proc status(res: HttpResponse; code: HttpCode): HttpResponse {.discardable,
    ...raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
Set the HTTP status code. 
proc stop(server: HttpServer) {....raises: [Exception], tags: [RootEffect],
                                forbids: [].}
Stop the HTTP server and close all connections 
proc streamFile(res: HttpResponse; path: string; req: HttpRequest;
                chunkSize = DefaultChunkSize) {....gcsafe,
    raises: [KeyError, OSError], tags: [], forbids: [].}

Stream a file for media playback with per-response byte limiting. Always process Range requests. Caps each response body to chunkSize bytes (default 1 MB) so a seek only transfers one chunk, not the entire remaining file. Always uses keep-alive.

No initial (no-Range) request sends 206 with Content-Range: bytes 0-(chunkSize-1)/fileSize — the browser learns the total file size from the suffix but only receives one chunk.

proc writeDisposition(buf: ptr UncheckedArray[byte]; name: string; p: var int) {.
    ...raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
